ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT COMMON COUNTS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. DEMURER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION TO ALLEGE IN CLEAR AND CONCISE LANGUAGE EACH ELEMENT OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THE EXECUTOR INCLUDING BASIS FOR LIABILITY. THE CAUSES OF ACTION SHALL ONLY BE ALLEGED AGAINST THE EXECUTOR IN BOTH THE CAPTION OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION AND IN THE BODY OF IT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. Appearances: Emilie Calhoun of Fiduciary Law Attorneys (415.759.8755) for Patricia Dark and Mary Grace Connolly on case number CGC-17-559572; Basil Plastiras of Plastiras & Terrizzi (415.472.8100) for Paul Thibeault on cases CGC-16-554534 and CGC-17-559572; J. Michael Phelps (415.569.4108) for Patricia Dark and Mary Grace Connolly on case CGC-16-554534.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts showing that common counts are not barred by the statute of limitations. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to all other causes of action to allege in clear and concise language each element of each cause of action against the executor including basis for liability. The causes of action shall only be alleged against the executor in both the caption of the cause of action and in the body of it. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Dawn Stark, CSR 7847 (US Legal Support, Tel: 415.362.4346) (501/REQ)
